summarization.a: The chart is an XRD pattern plotting diffracted intensity (arbitrary units) versus 2θ, showing the characteristic reflections of  PbSe ((200), (400), (220))  and PbTe ( (200), (400)) and the substrate Si peaks.

data_extraction.a:
| Measurement | Angle (2θ) | Intensity in 10^3 (arb.u) | hkl | Assignment |
|---|---|---|---|---|
|PbTe film for 2000 cycles |27.55 |15.3|200 | PbTe |
|PbTe film for 2000 cycles |32.89|1.7|-|Si|
|PbTe film for 2000 cycles |39.47|1.7|400|PbTe|
|PbTe film for 2000 cycles |56.85|2.33|220|PbTe|
|PbSe film for 2000 cycles|29.15|87.9|200| PbSe|
|PbSe film for 2000 cycles|32.95|1.39|-|Si|
|PbSe film for 2000 cycles|60.29|7.92|400|PbSe|

a.question: Why is PbSe’s signal so much stronger?
a.answer: PbSe appears to have higher crystallinity. Its XRD peaks are stronger and sharper than in PbTe sample. This indicates a larger fraction of well-ordered crystalline material.

a.question: Does PbSe have fcc or bcc structure?
a.answer: All reflections are even, which is consistent with fcc.

a.question: Does PbTe have fcc or bcc structure?
a.answer: All reflections are even, which is consistent with fcc.
